report_name: New Ransomware Group 'Genesis' Emerges, Claiming Attacks on Healthcare, Real Estate, and Tech Firms

threat_actors.name: Genesis
threat_actors.description: A new ransomware group that emerged in late 2025, employing a double extortion strategy. It targets small to mid-sized U.S. businesses in sectors like healthcare, real estate, and business services, listing victims on its dark web leak site.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion through a double extortion strategy, involving data exfiltration and file encryption.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The Genesis group deploys their ransomware to encrypt files across compromised systems as part of their double extortion strategy.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over Web Service
attack_patterns.description: The Genesis group exfiltrates large volumes of sensitive data from the victim's network before encryption.

attack_patterns.name: Phishing
attack_patterns.description: Believed to be a potential initial access vector for the Genesis ransomware group.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: The Genesis group is believed to use compromised credentials, possibly purchased from the dark web, to access remote services like RDP or VPN for initial access.

attack_patterns.name: Transfer Data to Cloud Account
attack_patterns.description: Sensitive files are collected, compressed, and exfiltrated to attacker-controlled cloud storage before encryption.

attack_patterns.name: Inhibit System Recovery
attack_patterns.description: The Genesis group likely deletes Volume Shadow Copies using tools like vssadmin to prevent system recovery from backups.

attack_patterns.name: OS Credential Dumping
attack_patterns.description: The group likely performs credential dumping from processes like lsass.exe to escalate privileges and move laterally.

attack_patterns.name: Permission Groups Discovery
attack_patterns.description: The group likely uses reconnaissance commands like 'net group "Domain Admins" /domain' to identify privileged accounts within the victim network.

malware.name: Genesis Ransomware
malware.description: The ransomware payload used by the Genesis group to encrypt files on victim systems as part of their double extortion attacks.

tools.name: rclone
tools.description: Used by the Genesis group for data exfiltration to attacker-controlled cloud storage.

tools.name: MegaSync
tools.description: Mentioned as a potential tool used by the Genesis group for data exfiltration.

tools.name: Mimikatz
tools.description: Mentioned as a potential tool for credential dumping activity.

identities.name: Dunagan Associates
identities.description: A U.S. real estate firm listed as a victim on the Genesis ransomware group's data leak site on July 5, 2026.

identities.name: East Texas Family Medicine
identities.description: A U.S. healthcare provider listed as a victim on the Genesis ransomware group's data leak site on July 5, 2026.

identities.name: Synergy Interactive
identities.description: A U.S. digital marketing agency listed as a victim on the Genesis ransomware group's data leak site on July 5, 2026.

infrastructure.name: Genesis Data Leak Site
infrastructure.description: A dark web site operated by the Genesis group to publish stolen data from victims who do not pay the ransom.

infrastructure.name: Genesis Exfiltration Storage
infrastructure.description: Attacker-controlled cloud storage used to stage and store data exfiltrated from victims.

indicators.name: Genesis Exfiltration Tool Usage
indicators.pattern:
title: Genesis Ransomware Exfiltration Tool Usage
id: 2a9c3b8e-1f7d-4b9a-8c6d-5e4f3a2b1c0d
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of legitimate file transfer tools like rclone.exe or megasync.exe, which are known to be used by the Genesis ransomware group for data exfiltration.
references:
  - https://www.comparitech.com/news/new-ransomware-gang-genesis-claims-9-data-breaches-including-optometrist-and-grocer/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-06
tags:
  - attack.t1537
  - attack.exfiltration
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\rclone.exe'
      - '\megasync.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the use of legitimate file transfer tools like rclone.exe or megasync.exe, which are associated with Genesis ransomware data exfiltration activities.

indicators.name: Genesis Credential Dumping
indicators.pattern:
title: Genesis Ransomware Credential Dumping Activity
id: 8d7c6b5a-4f3e-4d2c-1b0a-9e8f7d6c5b4a
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of mimikatz.exe, a tool used for credential dumping and associated with Genesis ransomware group's tactics.
references:
  - https://www.comparitech.com/news/new-ransomware-gang-genesis-claims-9-data-breaches-including-optometrist-and-grocer/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-06
tags:
  - attack.t1003
  - attack.credential_access
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\mimikatz.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of Mimikatz, a credential dumping tool hinted to be used by the Genesis group.

indicators.name: Genesis Reconnaissance Command
indicators.pattern:
title: Genesis Ransomware Reconnaissance Command
id: 5e4f3a2b-1c0d-4b9a-8c6d-2a9c3b8e1f7d
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of 'net group "Domain Admins"' command, a reconnaissance technique associated with the Genesis ransomware group to identify privileged accounts.
references:
  - https://www.comparitech.com/news/new-ransomware-gang-genesis-claims-9-data-breaches-including-optometrist-and-grocer/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-06
tags:
  - attack.t1069.002
  - attack.discovery
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'net'
      - 'group'
      - 'Domain Admins'
      - '/domain'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects reconnaissance commands used to identify privileged accounts, a tactic associated with the Genesis group.

indicators.name: Volume Shadow Copy Deletion
indicators.pattern:
title: Volume Shadow Copy Deletion via Vssadmin
id: 1b0a9e8f-7d6c-4b5a-8d7c-6b5a4f3e2d1c
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of vssadmin.exe to delete volume shadow copies, a common ransomware technique to inhibit system recovery.
references:
  - https://www.comparitech.com/news/new-ransomware-gang-genesis-claims-9-data-breaches-including-optometrist-and-grocer/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-06
tags:
  - attack.t1490
  - attack.impact
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\vssadmin.exe'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'delete'
      - 'shadows'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the deletion of Volume Shadow Copies using vssadmin, a common ransomware TTP to inhibit system recovery.

indicators.name: Genesis Tool Detection - rclone.exe
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Genesis Tool Detection - rclone.exe
id: 3b8e1f7d-2a9c-4b9a-8c6d-5e4f3a2b1c0e
status: experimental
description: Detects the presence of rclone.exe, a tool used by Genesis for exfiltration.
references:
  - https://www.comparitech.com/news/new-ransomware-gang-genesis-claims-9-data-breaches-including-optometrist-and-grocer/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-06
tags:
  - attack.t1537
level: medium
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith: '\rclone.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the presence of rclone.exe, a tool used by Genesis for exfiltration.
